1.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
1.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

1.gl: persuasion, i.e. credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher), especially reliance upon Christ for salvation; abstractly, constancy in such profession; by extension,
peace (literally or figuratively); by implication, prosperity
1.kj: one, peace, quietness, rest, + set at one again
1.dv: probably from a primary verb (to join);

2.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;
